title: xerodraba lycopodioides
intro: Xerodraba lycopodioides, also known as the New Zealand club moss, is a small, fern-like plant native to New Zealand. It is a type of lycopodiophyte, a group of plants that resemble mosses but are actually vascular plants. The medicinal properties of Xerodraba lycopodioides are still being researched, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Xerodraba lycopodioides is often used to support respiratory health and is said to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
